flashcards.front: What is an agent in AI?
flashcards.back: An agent is anything that can perceive its environment through sensors and act upon that environment through actuators.
Environment. It refers to the external context or world in which the agent operates.

A rational agent is one that, for each possible percept sequence, chooses an action that maximizes its expected performance measure, given the evidence from the percept sequence and its built-in knowledge.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between a Simple Reflex Agent and a Model-Based Reflex Agent?
flashcards.back: A Simple Reflex Agent acts only on the current percept. A Model-Based Reflex Agent maintains an internal state based on percept history, allowing it to operate in partially observable environments.

flashcards.front: Name the four main types of agents (from simple to complex).
flashcards.back: 1. Simple Reflex Agent, 2. Model-Based Reflex Agent, 3. Goal-Based Agent, 4. Utility-Based Agent. (Learning Agents are a separate category that can incorporate any of these).
